extracted_rules.class_0: R1: The series has multiple local peaks and valleys spread across the whole time span (no long, relatively flat segment); values keep fluctuating without staying nearly constant for more than a short interval.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: The series contains at least one long, relatively flat “plateau” where values remain roughly constant (minor noise only) before or after other ups-and-downs.

extracted_rules.class_0: R1: The series tends to oscillate within a mid-range (roughly between 0.2 and 0.7) without a single, dominant long downward or upward trend; instead it shows several medium-sized peaks and dips spread across the whole time span.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: The series typically features one prominent, relatively smooth large-scale peak followed by a marked, extended decline and recovery, creating a single “mountain-shaped” structure that dominates the overall shape.

extracted_rules.class_0: R1: The series has multiple distinct peaks and valleys spread across the whole time range, without a single dominant initial rise-and-fall; the amplitude fluctuates noticeably throughout, rather than mainly at the beginning.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: The series shows one dominant high peak very early, followed by an overall downward trend and smaller fluctuations later, so that the largest amplitude change occurs near the start.

extracted_rules.class_0: R1: The series has multiple medium-sized peaks and troughs spread across the whole length, without one single dominant early peak, and its average value tends to stay in the mid-range (roughly between 0.3 and 0.6) rather than sharply dropping low after an initial maximum.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: The series shows a strong, dominant peak very early, followed by a relatively steep decline to lower values and then smaller, less pronounced oscillations for the rest of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0: R1: After an initial high peak, the series shows a long, relatively flat middle section with moderate values (around 0.4–0.6) followed by several irregular ups and downs of similar moderate height, without any very deep, prolonged drop near the end.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: After an initial high peak, the series exhibits a strong, extended valley later in time: the values fall clearly lower than in the middle part (down toward ~0.1–0.2) and stay low for a noticeable span before partially recovering.

extracted_rules.class_0: R1: The series has multiple medium-sized peaks spread across the whole time range, with no single dominant peak that clearly stands out from the others.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: The series is dominated by one early high peak (near the start), after which values mostly stay lower and fluctuate without reaching a similarly high maximum again.

extracted_rules.class_0: R1: The series has multiple medium-sized peaks and valleys with at least one clear dip below 0.2 occurring after time 40.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: The series starts with a high value above 0.6 followed by a strong, mostly monotonic drop down toward about 0.2 within the first 10–15 time units.

extracted_rules.class_0: R1: The series has its largest peak in the first half (roughly before the midpoint), after which it generally decreases or fluctuates without forming a later peak of similar or larger height.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: The series has its largest peak in the second half (roughly after the midpoint), with earlier values not reaching as high as this later peak.

extracted_rules.class_0: R1: After the initial peak, the series stays mostly in a mid-to-low range (roughly between 0.2 and 0.6) with several moderate rises and falls, but without any long, steep monotonic drop from a high value down to near the minimum.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: Shortly after the start there is a long, steep monotonic decrease from a high value (above about 0.7) down close to the minimum (around 0.2 or lower) before the series fluctuates again.

extracted_rules.class_0: R1: The series has no very strong global downward trend: after the first 10–15 time steps, values typically stay in the middle range (around 0.3–0.6) without a long, steep drop from high (≈0.7–0.8) to low (≈0.2) that persists.
extracted_rules.class_1: R1: The series exhibits a strong early downward trend: starting near a high value (≈0.6–0.8), it drops steeply within the first 10–15 time steps to a much lower level (≈0.2 or below) and remains generally lower than it started for most of the remaining time.
